properties.notes:
Property demonstrates how moving back in resets the six-year absence rule clock, allowing multiple absence periods.

**Timeline:**
- 1 Sep 2008 to 1 Mar 2011: Main residence (2.5 years)
- 1 Mar 2011 to 1 Mar 2016: Rented - First absence (5 years - within 6-year limit)
- 1 Mar 2016 to 1 Jun 2018: Main residence again (2.25 years) - **RESETS CLOCK**
- 1 Jun 2018 to 1 Sep 2024: Rented - Second absence (6.25 years - exceeds 6-year limit)
- Total ownership: 16 years

**Key Calculations:**
- First absence: 5 years (within limit) - fully exempt
- Moving back in resets the six-year rule
- Second absence: 6.25 years (0.25 years exceeds limit)
- Taxable period: 0.25/16 years = 1.56% taxable
- Total capital gain: $345,000
- After time apportionment and 50% CGT discount
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $2,692**

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $2,692
- Only 0.25 years (3 months) of the second absence period is taxable

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-145 ITAA 1997 (Six-Year Absence Rule)
- Moving back in as main residence resets the six-year clock
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount)

user_query: Property at 38 University Drive, Kelvin Grove QLD 4059, multiple absence periods with reset when moved back in

additional_info.scenario_title: Six-Year Absence Rule - Multiple Periods with Reset
additional_info.expected_outcome: NET CAPITAL GAIN = $2,692 (only 0.25 years of second absence exceeds 6-year limit)
